prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of an electroplating cell. A power source connects a bar of plating metal labelled 'plating metal (anode, +)' and an object labelled 'object to be plated (cathode, −)', both dipped in a 'metal-salt bath'. Show metal ions migrating from anode to the object and a thin bright coat forming on the object. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Electroplating cell: object to be plated as cathode, a bar of plating metal as anode, both in a metal-salt solution, with metal depositing onto the object

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A glowing stream of electrons flows along a wire into an electrolysis cell; a digital counter above ticks upward (counting charge), and in lockstep a metal coating thickens visibly on the cathode. The idea: 'count the electrons, and you know exactly how much metal'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, amber electron stream and cyan deposit glow on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No readable text.
alt: A stream of electrons flowing through a cell with a counter ticking up, and metal steadily plating onto an electrode in proportion

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A long industrial 'pot line' of Hall–Héroult reduction cells glowing orange with molten aluminium, rows of thick carbon electrodes plunging in, heavy electrical busbars overhead carrying enormous current, sparks and heat haze. The mood is 'electricity forging metal at industrial scale'. Sleek cinematic-industrial style, molten-orange glow and cyan electrical accents on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A row of glowing industrial Hall-Héroult cells with molten aluminium and carbon electrodes, sparks and orange heat

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a Hall–Héroult aluminium cell. Several carbon anode blocks descend from a busbar at the top into a glowing 'molten Na₃AlF₆ + Al₂O₃' electrolyte. The cell floor is a carbon lining acting as the cathode; a layer of 'molten Al' pools on it with a tap-off spout on one side. Label: carbon anode (+), molten cryolite–alumina, molten Al (cathode floor, −), tap hole. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Hall-Héroult cell cross-section: carbon anodes from above into molten cryolite-alumina electrolyte, molten aluminium layer at the bottom on a carbon cathode lining, tap hole

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a chlor-alkali diaphragm cell. A central anode (graphite) generating 'Cl₂' bubbles, surrounded by a porous 'asbestos diaphragm', enclosed by an outer 'iron-mesh cathode' producing 'H₂' bubbles. Brine ('NaCl') fed in at the top arrow; 'dilute NaOH' drips out the cathode side to a reservoir. Label: anode (Cl₂), diaphragm, iron cathode (H₂ + NaOH), brine in. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Diaphragm cell: central anode generating Cl2, surrounded by a porous asbestos diaphragm and an iron-mesh cathode; brine fed at top, dilute NaOH and H2 collected at cathode side

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). An array of everyday batteries laid out — cylindrical AA/AAA dry cells, a small silver button cell, and a chunky car battery — each with a faint internal glow suggesting stored chemical energy and tiny electron streams along their terminals. The mood is 'portable chemical power, all around us'. Clean photorealistic-technical style, cool cyan/amber electrical glow on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A spread of everyday batteries — AA cells, a button cell, a car battery — glowing with stored chemical energy

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a), cutaway of a cylindrical dry cell. Show the outer 'zinc shell (anode, −)', a central 'graphite rod (cathode, +)', the surrounding moist 'paste of MnO₂, NH₄Cl, carbon', a 'porous separator', and the sealed top. Label each part. Orange accent labels and thin leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Cutaway of a cylindrical dry cell showing the outer zinc shell (anode), a central graphite rod (cathode), and the surrounding MnO2 + NH4Cl paste with a porous separator

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a), cutaway of a small button (coin) cell. Show the 'Zn(Hg) anode (−)' at top, '45% KOH on absorbent', a 'barrier/separator', 'HgO + graphite cathode (+)' at the bottom, an 'insulating gasket', and the 'outer steel case'. Label each part. Orange accent labels and thin leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Cutaway of a small button-shaped mercury cell showing the zinc amalgam anode, HgO mixed with graphite cathode, 45% KOH on an absorbent, an insulating gasket and an outer steel case

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Left: a chunky black car lead-acid battery; centre: a smartphone glowing; right: a hybrid-car battery module — with curved arrows of electrons flowing both ways to suggest charge and discharge. The mood is 'energy you can put back'. Clean photorealistic-technical style, cool cyan and warm amber glow on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A car lead-acid battery beside a smartphone and a hybrid-car battery pack, electrons cycling back and forth to suggest recharging

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of one cell of a lead-acid battery: interleaved vertical 'Pb (anode, −)' and 'PbO₂ (cathode, +)' plates dipping into 'H₂SO₄ electrolyte (38%)', with terminals on top. Add small arrows labelled 'discharge' and 'charge' to show reversibility. Label the plates and electrolyte. Orange accent labels and thin leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Cutaway of one cell of a lead storage battery with alternating plates of Pb (anode) and PbO2 (cathode) immersed in H2SO4 electrolyte

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a), two side-by-side panels of a lithium-ion cell. LEFT 'Charge': a charging device drives electrons one way while 'Li⁺' ions move through the electrolyte into the 'graphite' electrode (becoming Li⁺ₓ-graphite); the other electrode is 'LiCoO₂'. RIGHT 'Discharge': a load draws electrons the other way and Li⁺ ions flow back toward the 'Li₁₋ₓCoO₂' electrode. Label both electrodes, electron-flow arrows, and Li⁺ paths. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Two-panel diagram of a lithium-ion cell: charge cycle with Li+ moving into graphite, and discharge cycle with Li+ moving back to the LiCoO2 electrode, electrons flowing through the external circuit

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Streams of H₂ and O₂ gas flowing into a glowing fuel-cell stack that powers a bright bulb, with a wisp of water vapour as the only exhaust. The mood is 'clean electricity from gas, no smoke'. Clean photorealistic-technical style, cool cyan electrical glow and a soft white vapour plume on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: Hydrogen and oxygen streaming into a fuel cell that lights a bulb, with only water vapour leaving

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a hydrogen–oxygen fuel cell. 'H₂ gas in' at the right 'anode (−)', 'O₂ in' at the left 'cathode (+)', both 'porous carbon electrodes', a central 'KOH electrolyte' showing 'OH⁻' migrating, a bulb glowing in the external circuit, and 'H₂O + water vapour out'. Label electrodes, gas feeds, electrolyte and ion flow. Orange accent labels and thin leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Diagram of an H2-O2 fuel cell with H2 gas fed to the anode, O2 to the cathode, porous carbon electrodes, KOH electrolyte with OH- ions, a bulb in the external circuit, and water vapour leaving

prompt: Technical illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) of a PEM fuel cell cross-section. From left to right: 'flow field', 'catalyst layer', a central yellow 'proton-exchange membrane' with 'H⁺' crossing it, another catalyst layer, 'porous gas-diffusion layer'. 'H₂' fed at the 'anode (−)' splitting to '2H⁺ + 2e⁻'; 'O₂' at the 'cathode (+)' forming 'H₂O'; electrons 'e⁻' shown leaving through the external circuit. Label membrane, electrodes, gas feeds and water out. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: PEM fuel cell cross-section showing flow fields, catalyst layers, a central proton-exchange membrane carrying H+, gas diffusion layers, with H2 fed to the anode and O2 to the cathode and water leaving
